name: The5ers
description_short: Forex-focused prop firm with scaling up to $4M, multiple program types, and profit splits up to 100%.
description_long:
## The $4M Scaling Promise Is the Whole Pitch

The5ers built its brand around one number: $4 million. Nearly every prop firm dangles a maximum funded balance, but The5ers has kept the $4M scaling ceiling front and center since it launched in 2016, and that ceiling is the reason serious forex traders keep this firm on their shortlist rather than defaulting to FTMO or the newer challenge-heavy shops. The trade-off is that reaching anywhere near that number is a multi-year commitment that depends on consistent monthly performance across a specific rule set, not a quick evaluation sprint.

This review looks at The5ers as it is priced and structured today, focusing on the decisions a trader actually has to make: which program, which capital tier, and whether the firm's forex-heavy identity fits the way you actually trade. The5ers sits in the prop-firms category alongside FTMO, Topstep and MyForexFunds successors, but its long-tenured reputation and scaling model make it a genuinely different bet.

## The Three-Program Choice That Decides Your Cost

The5ers does not sell a single product. The current lineup splits into Hyper Growth, High Stakes and a 1-Step Bootcamp variant, and the choice between them is the single biggest cost decision you will make before funding a dollar.

Hyper Growth starts at **$95 for the $6,000 account** and steps up to **$275 for the $20,000 tier**. There is no evaluation phase in the traditional sense on the small Hyper Growth accounts; you begin trading real capital under strict daily and total drawdown rules, and the scaling milestones unlock larger balances over months. High Stakes is the more familiar prop-firm structure: the **$100,000 account costs $575**, requires a 6% profit target, and pays an **80/20 split** with the trader taking the larger share.

The scraped 1-Step Bootcamp page shows a different variant priced at **$249** for a $100K equivalent, with a 10% target, 6% max loss, 3% max daily loss, unlimited trading period, a 75/25 profit share and a **$2,000 payout cap** on the funded stage. The payout cap is easy to miss and matters: it caps how much you can withdraw per cycle regardless of how much you make above it.

Deciding between these programs comes down to how you want to prove yourself. Hyper Growth is patient capital for traders who trust their edge over 30 to 90 days. High Stakes is for traders who want the industry-standard challenge-and-fund path with a bigger starting balance. The 1-Step is the middle ground with its own payout ceiling.

## Rules That Punish Impatience

Every funded tier the source material discloses runs a **6% maximum overall loss and a 3% maximum daily loss**. Those numbers are tighter than several competitors that allow 5% daily and 10% total, and this is where new prop traders wash out of The5ers most often. A single revenge-trade session that pushes 3% intraday ends the account, no reset available without repurchasing.

There is also a **50% per-day consistency rule** in the disclosed program tables, meaning no single day can account for more than half of your total profit. In practice, this blocks the strategy of hitting one lucky news trade and coasting to a payout. You need at least two productive days for every profitable cycle, which structurally favors swing traders and daily-timeframe systems over scalpers who rely on rare, oversized wins.

The leverage is disclosed as **1:100**, which is standard for FX but noticeably lower than the 1:200 or 1:500 some newer firms offer. If your strategy depends on high leverage on exotic pairs, The5ers will feel restrictive.

## What The5ers Does Not Give You

The firm markets itself on capital and community, not on tools. The tool profile flags that The5ers does not ship with **backtesting, a mobile app, journaling, alerts, custom indicators, an API, broker-integration widgets or a news feed of its own**. You bring your own platform, your own analytics and your own journal. Automated trading is permitted on some plans and social features exist inside the trader community, but nothing here replaces TradingView, Edgewonk, Chartlog or whatever you already use.

That is not a criticism so much as a scope note. If you were hoping the fee covers a research suite, it does not. The5ers is a capital provider and rule enforcer, and it expects you to be self-sufficient on the technology stack.

## Where the Forex Bias Really Bites

The tool data lists **forex, stocks and crypto** as covered markets, and the site now shows separate CFD and Futures tabs, but the firm's DNA is currency trading. Marketing content, trader stories, education material and the drawdown rules are all calibrated for FX pairs. Stock CFD spreads and crypto conditions are not the flagship offering, and the community's expertise is heavily skewed toward majors and crosses.

If you are a futures-first trader looking at CME products like ES, NQ or CL, Topstep, Apex or TakeProfitTrader are calibrated for you in a way The5ers is not, even if The5ers now lists a futures tab. If your edge is in equities or crypto specifically, most reviewers will steer you elsewhere. Where The5ers earns its keep is with the FX swing trader who wants a scaling relationship with one funder over years.

## Reading the Trader-Story Payouts Honestly

The homepage carousels prominently featured payouts ranging from **$3,507 to $36,005**, with several traders in the $13K to $22K range. These are real, cited payouts, but they represent selected success stories from a firm operating since 2016 with tens of thousands of accounts. They tell you the payout mechanism works and that meaningful sums do get released; they do not tell you the pass rate or the median outcome, and The5ers, like every prop firm, does not publish that data.

Read these numbers as proof-of-payment, not as an expected value. The realistic mental model is that a small percentage of traders pass, a smaller percentage stay funded past six months, and a still smaller percentage reach the scaling milestones that unlock the larger balances the marketing highlights.

## The Trader This Firm Actually Fits

The5ers is a strong fit if you are a **disciplined FX trader with an existing edge on the daily or 4-hour chart**, you can tolerate a 3% daily and 6% total drawdown, and you are willing to think in six-to-twelve-month arcs rather than one-week challenges. The Hyper Growth model rewards traders who compound slowly and consistently, and the scaling ladder means your best year is not capped at $200K of buying power the way it is at most single-tier firms.

The5ers is a poor fit if you are a **high-frequency scalper, a news-event trader, an equity or futures specialist, or someone who wants a one-off challenge and payout without an ongoing relationship**. It is also poor fit if you cannot handle a total account loss with no cheap retry — the fee to restart is the full program price again.

## Comparing The5ers to the Obvious Alternatives

Against **FTMO**, The5ers offers a longer time horizon and higher scaling ceiling but tighter daily drawdown and a smaller product range. FTMO's evaluation is more standardized and its payout infrastructure has a longer public track record on Trustpilot at scale.

Against **Topstep** and futures-first firms, The5ers is not the right tool if your instrument is /ES or /NQ. Topstep's rule set and trader development pipeline are built for futures traders in a way The5ers is not.

Against newer entrants like **FundingPips, FundedNext or Alpha Capital**, The5ers trades marketing flash for tenure. It has been paying out since 2016 across market cycles that ended other firms. That longevity is the specific thing you are paying for when you choose The5ers over a cheaper, newer challenge.

## The Decision, Compressed

Buy The5ers if you are an FX swing trader who wants a long-term funded relationship with a firm that has survived the prop-firm shakeout. Skip it if you trade futures or equities primarily, or if you need a fast challenge-to-payout cycle without a multi-month scaling commitment. The **$95 Hyper Growth entry** is the low-risk way to test whether the rule set fits your strategy before committing to the $575 High Stakes program.
pros: $4M scaling ceiling is higher than most competing prop firms
Operating since 2016, longer track record than most competitors
Multiple program types (Hyper Growth, High Stakes, 1-Step) fit different styles
Hyper Growth $6K entry at $95 lowers the cost of testing the rule set
Profit splits reach up to 100% at higher scaling stages
No time limit on trading period in disclosed 1-Step and funded tiers
cons: 3% max daily loss is tighter than several competitors
1-Step funded stage disclosed a $2,000 payout cap per cycle
50% daily consistency rule punishes single big-win strategies
No built-in journaling, backtesting, mobile app or news feed
Forex-centric identity means futures and equity traders get a weaker experience
best_for: FX swing traders on daily and 4-hour timeframes
Traders wanting a multi-year scaling relationship, not a one-off challenge
Disciplined traders comfortable with 3% daily and 6% total drawdown
Traders who already own their charting and journaling stack
Consistent performers targeting balances above the $200K single-tier cap
meta_title: The5ers Review 2026: $4M Scaling for FX Traders
meta_description: Honest The5ers review for 2026. Hyper Growth from $95, High Stakes at $575, $4M scaling, 3% daily drawdown. Who this forex prop firm actually fits.
company_background: The5ers Ltd. is a proprietary trading firm founded in 2016 and headquartered in Raanana, Israel. It focuses on forex-first funded trading programs with a scaling model that advertises up to $4 million in maximum funded capital. The firm is one of the older continuously operating prop firms, predating the 2020-era boom that produced most current competitors.
scraped_features: Hyper Growth $6K account for $95 entry fee
6% maximum overall loss on disclosed tiers
3% maximum daily loss on disclosed tiers
Unlimited trading period on 1-Step and funded stages
Profit splits scaling up to 100% at higher tiers
Fee refund from third payout on 1-Step
Multi-currency account options (USD, EUR, GBP, INR)
